name: Piedrabuena and Tabla de la Y
name_original: Piedrabuena y Tabla de la Yedra — circular desde Puebla de Don Rodrigo
description: This difficult 66.7-mile road cycling route from Puebla de Don Rodrigo explores Piedrabuena and the scenic Tabla de la Yedra.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Puebla de Don Rodrigo traverse a diverse landscape within the province of Ciudad Real, Castile-La Mancha. The region features valleys, rolling hills, and river areas, including sections along the Guadiana River. Cyclists can expect to ride through environments characterized by holm oak dehesas and varied natural spaces, offering a range of terrain for road cycling. Best road cycling routes around Puebla de Don Rodrigo The most popular road cycling route is Piedrabuena and Tabla de la Y, a 66.7 miles (107.4 km) trail that takes 5 hours 22 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers varied terrain through the local countryside. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Abenójar, Saceruela, and Luciana Loop, a difficult 72.0 miles (116.0 km) path. This route provides extensive views of the surrounding natural landscapes and rural areas. Local road cyclists also love the Saceruela y Los Pozuelos de Calatrava desde Piedrabuena – circular por Luciana y Tabla de la Yedra, a 81.5 miles (131.1 km) trail leading through diverse rural and natural environments, often completed in about 6 hours 2 minutes. Road cycling around Puebla de Don Rodrigo is defined by valleys, holm oak dehesas, and river gorges. The network offers options for different ability levels, from less-trafficked rural roads to more challenging routes with significant elevation changes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 40 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Puebla de Don Rodrigo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Puebla de Don Rodrigo?
A: There are 6 road cycling routes documented on komoot for the Puebla de Don Rodrigo region. These routes offer a variety of experiences, from moderate to difficult.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of road cycling routes in Puebla de Don Rodrigo?
A: The road cycling routes in Puebla de Don Rodrigo are predominantly challenging. Out of the 6 routes, 5 are classified as difficult, and 1 is moderate. This indicates that most routes are suited for experienced cyclists looking for significant elevation changes and longer distances.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Puebla de Don Rodrigo?
A: Cyclists can expect a diverse landscape featuring valleys, rolling hills, and river areas, including sections along the Guadiana River. The routes often traverse environments characterized by holm oak dehesas, farmlands, and dramatic gorges like El Chiquero and Estrecho de las Hoces del Guadiana, offering varied natural scenery.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes in the region are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1251040932/abenojar-saceruela-and-luciana-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abenójar, Saceruela, and Luciana Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 116 km path offering extensive views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086215201/saceruela-y-los-pozuelos-de-calatrava-desde-piedrabuena-circular-por-luciana-y-tabla-de-la-yedra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saceruela y Los Pozuelos de Calatrava desde Piedrabuena – circular por Luciana y Tabla de la Yedra&lt;/a&gt;, a 131.1 km route through diverse rural and natural environments.

Q: What natural landmarks can I see along road cycling routes in Puebla de Don Rodrigo?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You might encounter the impressive gorge of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6319154" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Chiquero (Guadiana Trails GR 114)&lt;/a&gt;, where the Guadiana River carves through 100-meter-high walls. Other notable areas include the pastures of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5880050" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Raña de las Peñas Lisas dehesas&lt;/a&gt; and the unique ecosystems of the Microrreservas de Bonales. The nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6517037" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cíjara Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt; also offers beautiful forest landscapes.

Q: Are there any historical sites or points of interest near the cycling routes?
A: Yes, beyond the natural landscapes, you can explore historical sites. The town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5620043" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puebla de Don Rodrigo&lt;/a&gt; itself features the 15th-century Iglesia Parroquial de San Juan Bautista. Just outside the town, you can also see the 19th-century industrial Puente de Retama.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Puebla de Don Rodrigo?
A: While specific recommendations vary, generally, spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling in this part of Spain. Temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer months can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable.

Q: How long do road cycling routes typically take in this region?
A: The duration of road cycling routes in Puebla de Don Rodrigo varies significantly based on distance and elevation. For instance, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20128554" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Ríofrío – Puebla de Don Rodrigo loop&lt;/a&gt;, covering 88.2 km, typically takes around 4 hours 24 minutes. Longer, more difficult routes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1371422513/piedrabuena-and-tabla-de-la-y" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piedrabuena and Tabla de la Y&lt;/a&gt; (107.4 km), can take over 5 hours, while the 131.2 km &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067776381/villarta-de-los-montes-from-puebla-de-don-rodrigo-circular-by-embalse-de-cijara-and-horcajo-de-los-montes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villarta de los Montes from Puebla de Don Rodrigo – circular by Embalse de Cijara and Horcajo de los Montes&lt;/a&gt; may require over 6 hours.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options in Puebla de Don Rodrigo?
A: The road cycling routes listed for Puebla de Don Rodrigo are generally long and challenging, with most classified as difficult and only one as moderate. This suggests that the area is better suited for experienced adult cyclists. For families, it might be more appropriate to look for shorter, flatter sections of local roads or consider mountain biking trails if available, which often have easier segments.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Puebla de Don Rodrigo?
A: The komoot community appreciates the varied terrain and natural beauty of the region. Cyclists often praise the scenic roads that wind through holm oak dehesas, along the Guadiana River, and through impressive gorges. The low traffic on many rural roads also contributes to a tranquil and enjoyable cycling experience.

Q: Are there options for parking near the road cycling routes?
A: While specific parking areas for each route are not detailed, Puebla de Don Rodrigo is a rural municipality. Cyclists typically find parking in or near the main town squares or designated public parking areas within the villages that serve as starting points for routes. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.
tips:
Municipality located in the valley of the Tierteafuera river. In the middle of the Guadiana union basin, framed and partially divided by the Grande and Chico streams, 2 km from the Guadiana river course.
There is evidence of habitation in this term since pre-Roman times, as evidenced by the presence of several castellars, such as the one in the Castle or the one in Castilnegro, and even a motilla found in the 70s and located at the exit of the town .

In the western part of the province and the historic Campo de Calatrava, between the Canalizos and San Antón mountain ranges and the Guadiana valley, with very rugged relief and evocative landscapes, is the pleasant town of Luciana.

It houses the mouths of two tributaries of the Guadiana River: the Bullaque in the northeast, very close to the town, and the Tirteafuera in the southwest, in Tablacaldera. Water, always very present, gives uniqueness and beauty to the environment.
Nice park at the entrance of Piedrabuena, in the center a monument in honor of the people who maintain the tradition of the May Crosses.

Located in the Maiyor square next to the Town Hall. It is a Neoclassical style construction. Among the construction materials, brick predominates, and of course volcanic stone, and the metallic roofs of the towers stand out. Very nice in general everything.

As it passes through Piedrabuena, the Bullaque River has an area of calm waters known as the Tabla de la Yedra. This is a place of recreation that is visited annually by thousands of people, being one of the most outstanding spaces equipped for bathing in the province of Ciudad Real.
Pedaling along the CR-721 road, you reach Arroba de los Montes. A small rural town on the slopes of the Sierra de los Frailes. A good place to stop for a coffee and rest from the route.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as difficult, covering a distance of approximately 107 km with an elevation gain of about 1130 meters. It is suitable for road cycling enthusiasts looking for a challenging ride.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The route features quiet, well-paved roads with gentle hills, characteristic of the local landscape. You will also encounter long straight stretches in some sections, allowing for a good pace. The terrain offers a varied experience for road cyclists.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the starting point in Puebla de Don Rodrigo, the Los Ejidos de Luciana Recreational Area, and the Piedrabuena Park. A significant natural highlight is the Tabla de la Yedra Recreation Area, known for its stunning views and tranquil waters of the Bullaque River.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region's climate suggests that spring and autumn are ideal for road cycling, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summers can be very hot, and winters might be chilly, so plan accordingly for comfortable conditions.

faqs.question: Where can I park when starting this route from Puebla de Don Rodrigo?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for cyclists in Puebla de Don Rodrigo are not provided, as a known starting point for outdoor routes, you can typically find public parking within the town. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Road cycling routes are generally not ideal for dogs due to the pace and potential traffic. If you wish to bring a dog, it's recommended to stick to designated walking or hiking trails where dogs are permitted, and always keep them on a leash.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No specific permits are generally required for road cycling on public roads in the Castilla-La Mancha region. However, always respect local regulations and private property signs, especially if you deviate from the main route.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, towns like Puebla de Don Rodrigo, Arroba de los Montes, and Piedrabuena offer various amenities including cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options suitable for cyclists. The Tabla de la Yedra Recreation Area is also a popular spot for a break.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and views can I expect?
faqs.answer: You can expect diverse natural landscapes, from tree-lined sections near Puebla de Don Rodrigo to the typical vegetation around Piedrabuena. The Tabla de la Yedra is a natural paradise with stunning views, particularly where the Bullaque River creates calm waters.

faqs.question: Is this a circular route?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is designed as a circular loop, starting and ending in Puebla de Don Rodrigo, allowing you to experience the full journey without retracing your steps.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route briefly intersect with other paths. You will encounter parts of the Camino Natural del Guadiana and the Luciana - río Guadiana - El Chiquero trail, among others.
